Curriculum Digests Newsletter

Chapter 3 Awareness

Visibility in the Age of Agents

⏱️ 60 minutes

🎯 Learning Objectives

  • Understand how AI agents discover and rank vendors
  • Learn the difference between SEO and GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)
  • Build structured content that LLMs can parse and cite
  • Test and measure your AI visibility

How Agents Find You

When a buyer asks ChatGPT "What are the best enterprise data platforms?", what happens next determines whether you exist in their consideration set.

AI agents don't browse websites like humans. They don't click through your navigation, skim your hero banner, or download your whitepaper. Instead, they:

  • Query training data — Knowledge baked into the model from web crawls, documentation, and public content
  • Retrieve real-time information — RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pulls from live sources
  • Synthesize and rank — Combine multiple sources into a coherent answer with implicit rankings

Key Insight

You're not optimizing for a buyer to find your website. You're optimizing for an AI to mention you when asked about your category — and to describe you accurately when it does.

The Three Layers of Agent Discovery

📚 Training Data

Content that was crawled before the model's knowledge cutoff. Wikipedia, major publications, documentation, widely-cited sources. Hard to influence directly, but authoritative content eventually makes it in.

🔍 Real-Time Retrieval

RAG-enabled queries pull from live web sources. Your current website, recent press, updated documentation. This is where GEO tactics have immediate impact.

🔗 Entity Relationships

How you're connected to known entities — customers, partners, analysts, industry terms. Agents use knowledge graphs to validate claims and establish credibility.

From SEO to GEO

For two decades, we optimized for Google's algorithm. Keywords, backlinks, page speed, mobile-friendliness. That playbook doesn't disappear, but it's now table stakes — not the game.

GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is the new discipline. It's about structuring your content so AI agents can parse it, quote it, and cite it accurately.

SEO Mindset

  • Optimize for keywords
  • Build backlinks
  • Drive clicks to website
  • Compete for SERP position
  • Measure rankings

GEO Mindset

  • Optimize for entity recognition
  • Build knowledge graph presence
  • Get cited in AI responses
  • Compete for mention quality
  • Measure AI visibility

The GEO Playbook

📋 Schema.org Markup

Structured data that explicitly tells agents what your content means. Product schemas, organization data, FAQ markup, How-To content. This is machine-readable context.

🏢 Entity Establishment

Ensure your company and products are recognized entities. Wikipedia presence, Wikidata entries, consistent naming across the web. Agents need to know you exist as a distinct thing.

📝 Quotable Claims

Clear, specific statements that agents can excerpt. "SAP processes $46 trillion in commerce annually" is quotable. "We're a leading platform" is not.

🔗 Authoritative Linking

Citations from recognized sources. Analyst mentions, press coverage, academic references. Agents weight authoritative sources more heavily.

Content That Agents Quote

When an AI responds to "Tell me about SAP's data management capabilities," it's pulling fragments from multiple sources and synthesizing an answer. Your job is to make sure the fragments it pulls are accurate, differentiated, and favorable.

The Quotability Test

For each key claim you want agents to surface, ask:

  • Is it specific? Numbers, names, concrete facts beat vague superlatives
  • Is it standalone? Does the sentence make sense out of context?
  • Is it unique? Could a competitor say the same thing?
  • Is it verifiable? Can the claim be checked against other sources?

Before & After

Weak: "We're the leading enterprise data management platform with best-in-class capabilities."

Strong: "SAP Datasphere connects to 150+ data sources and processes over 2 billion transactions daily for 25,000 enterprise customers."

Structuring for Extraction

  • Lead with the conclusion — Put your key claim in the first sentence, not the last
  • Use clear headers — Agents use headers to understand content hierarchy
  • Create definitive statements — "X is..." beats "X can be described as..."
  • Include comparison anchors — "Unlike traditional ETL tools, Datasphere provides..."

Measuring AI Visibility

You can't improve what you don't measure. AI visibility testing should become a regular practice, just like tracking search rankings.

The AI Visibility Audit

Systematically query multiple AI tools about your product category and document what they surface:

🤖 ChatGPT

Query with and without web browsing enabled. Note differences in responses. Check knowledge cutoff impact.

🔮 Perplexity

Strong real-time retrieval. Check citations — are you being sourced? What sources outrank you?

🎭 Claude

Different training corpus. May have different view of your market. Compare to ChatGPT responses.

🔍 Gemini

Google's integration with Search. How does AI response differ from traditional SERP?

What to Query

  • Category queries: "Best enterprise data platforms" / "Top CDP vendors"
  • Comparison queries: "SAP vs Snowflake for data management"
  • Problem queries: "How to unify customer data across systems"
  • Specific queries: "What is SAP Datasphere?" / "SAP data management capabilities"
Document every response. Track changes over time. Your AI visibility is now as important as your search visibility.

🎯 Interactive Exercise: AI Visibility Audit

Complete this exercise to assess your current AI visibility:

  1. Choose 3 category queries relevant to your product (e.g., "best enterprise data platforms")
  2. Query each across 3 AI tools (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude)
  3. Document for each response:
    • Are you mentioned? In what position?
    • How are you described? Is it accurate?
    • What competitors are mentioned alongside you?
    • What sources are cited (if visible)?
  4. Identify gaps: Where should you appear but don't?
  5. Create action items: What content changes would improve your visibility?

Time: ~20 minutes

Key Takeaways

  • AI agents discover vendors through training data, real-time retrieval, and entity relationships
  • GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is the new discipline — structured content that agents can parse and cite
  • Schema.org markup, entity establishment, and quotable claims are your GEO foundation
  • Content must be specific, standalone, unique, and verifiable to get quoted
  • Regular AI visibility audits across multiple tools should be standard practice
